exercises.name: Medicine Ball Pike Push Up
exercises.description:
Begin in a pike position with your hips raised and feet close together. 

Place your hands on a medicine ball directly under your shoulders. 

Lower your head toward the ball by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a V-shape, then push back up. 

This targets your shoulders and core stability.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Pistol Squat
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure to keep the weight is in front of you. It will help counter-balance your body weight, and help you get back up.

2) You can go to an easier progression such as doing a single leg squat with the TRX straps.

3) Pick up a kettlebell with two hands and hold it by the horns upside down. Hold one leg off of the floor and squat down on the other.

4) Squat down by flexing the knee and sitting back with the hips, and extending both hands forward while holding the kettlebell.

5) Once at the bottom position, reverse the motion driving through the heel while keeping your head and chest up.

6) Lower yourself again and repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Plank
exercises.description: Medicine Ball Plank

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Plank Roll Outs
exercises.description: In a plank position, place forearms on a medicine ball. Roll it forward slightly, then return to start.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Place your hands on top of the ball and your body at a 45-degree angle with the floor, balancing yourself on the tips of your toes.

2.Bend at the elbows and lower your upper body to a few inches from the ball.

3.Push yourself back up to the starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Push Up to Mountain Climber
exercises.description:
Start in a high plank position with both hands on a medicine ball. Lower your chest into a push-up, then explosively push back up. Immediately drive one knee toward your chest for a single mountain climber. 

On the next rep, perform the push-up again and switch to the opposite leg for the mountain climber. 

Continue alternating legs with each rep while maintaining a strong plank and engaged core throughout.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Rainbows
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball with both hands in front of your hips. 

With your arms extended, explosively swing the ball in a wide arc up and over your head toward one side of your body, then immediately reverse the motion to bring it over to the opposite side. 

Keep your core tight and rotate through your torso and hips with power, maintaining a strong, athletic stance throughout the movement. Continue alternating sides in a fast and controlled rhythm.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest.

2.Take a step backward, dropping your back knee toward the floor, keeping the ball against your chest.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Reverse Lunge & Twist
exercises.description:
1.Take a step forward, dropping your back knee toward the floor, then, holding a ball with both hands, push extending arms straight out in front of you.

2.Twist on the side of your knee that is not touching the floor.

3.Push off your front foot returning to starting position.

4.Repeat and alternate.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Russian Twist
exercises.alternateName: med ball v sit core twists
exercises.description:
Sit on the floor with a medicine ball in both hands. 

Lean back slightly and twist your torso from side to side, tapping the ball on the floor next to each hip.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Shoulder Toss
exercises.description:
Hold a medicine ball in front of you at shoulder height. 

Toss the ball to the side, rotating your torso, and catch it as it returns, repeating the motion on both sides.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Sit-Up To Press
exercises.alternateName: med ball full crunch to shoulder press
exercises.description: Perform a sit-up while holding a medicine ball. As you come up, press the ball overhead, then lower back down and repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Skaters
exercises.description: Hold a medicine ball and perform a skater jump, leaping side to side while moving the ball across your body with each jump.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Slam
exercises.description:
"1.Stand upright holding a medicine ball with both hands and stand with your feet at shoulder width.

2.Raise the ball above your head, arms fully extended and straight.

3.Slam the ball into the ground directly in front of you as hard as you can.

4.Catch the ball with both hands on the bounce and repeat the movement."

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Slam with Squat Jump
exercises.description:
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a medicine ball overhead. 

Slam the ball down forcefully while bending into a squat. Pick up the ball and immediately jump straight up, holding the ball overhead. 

Land softly and repeat. This combines explosive power and full-body coordination.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Slider Lunge
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your abdominals on. Ensure that all your lower body segments hit about 90 degrees at the bottom of the lunge.

2.You’ll also wanna make sure that your front knee doesn’t pass your toes on that foot. 

3.With your feet on the sliders, carry the medicine ball close to your chest. This will be your starting position.

4.Slowly extend one foot backwards until knee is close to the ground and the other knee is at 90 degrees. Then pull yourself back to starting position.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

6.When you complete one side, then do the other.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest.

2.Bend at your knees, putting yourself in a squat position.

3.Straighten legs returning to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Squat & Press
exercises.description:
1.Holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest, take a squat, feet about shoulder-width apart.

2.Stand straight keeping legs straight, as you do this, press the ball up over your head, arms straight and extended.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Squat & Throw
exercises.description:
1.Holding the ball with both hands, just under the chin and against your chest, take a squat, feet about shoulder-width apart.

2.Stand straight keeping legs straight, as you do this, throw the ball up.

3.Return to a squat position, as you do this, catch the ball.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Staggered Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Keeping your back flat, support your body with your feet on the ground, one hand on a med ball, the other on the floor, arms straight.

2.Lower your body, with your chest almost touching the floor, elbows bent.

3.Push up to a straight arm position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Twist
exercises.description:
1.Holding a medicine ball, stand upright with arms extended straight out in front of you.

2.Bring the ball to one side, twisting at your torso, keeping arms straight.

3.Bring the ball to the other side.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball V-up
exercises.description:
Hold a medicine ball in both hands. Bring one knee up towards your chest while snapping the ball towards the knee.

Alternate sides in a controlled motion.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball V-Up Pass (Feet to Hands)
exercises.description:
Lie flat on your back, holding a medicine ball overhead with your arms extended and legs straight. Engage your core and lift your legs and upper body simultaneously, bringing them toward each other to form a “V” shape. 

As they meet, pass the medicine ball from your hands to your feet, holding it between your ankles. 

Lower your arms and legs back down without letting them touch the floor, then repeat the motion, passing the ball back to your hands on the next rep.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Windshield Wiper Bent Legs
exercises.description: Lie on your back with bent knees holding a medicine ball. Rotate your legs side to side while keeping your upper body stable.

exercises.name: Mini Band Alternating Hip Abduction
exercises.description:
1. Start by placing the Mini Band around your ankles. Keep your knees just slightly bent.

2. While keeping your knees in line with your toes, reach one foot out to the side. Step back together, then reach the opposite foot out. 

3. Repeat this movement, with knees slightly bent, while trying not to move up and down with your body.

exercises.name: Mini Band Alternating Hip Flexion
exercises.description:
Stand with a mini band around your feet.

Alternate lifting each knee toward your chest while keeping tension on the band. 

Move with control to engage your hip flexors.

exercises.name: Mini Band Alternating Lateral Lunge
exercises.description:
1. Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart. Place the band around both ankles. 

2. Step your foot out towards your right side and reach your hips back to control your descent. 

3. Push yourself back up to a standing position, then repeat on the opposite side. 

4. Continue alternating sides.

exercises.name: Mini Band Alternating Side Steps
exercises.description:
1. Start by placing the Mini Band around your ankles. Keep your knees just slightly bent.

2. While keeping your knees in line with your toes, reach one foot out to the side. Step your opposite foot towards the first foot. 

3. Move in a slow and controlled fashion while trying not to move up and down with your body. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Mini Band Alternating Side Steps (Squat Stance)
exercises.description:
1. Start by placing the Mini Band around your ankles, and descending into a half-squat position. 

2. While keeping your knees in line with your toes, reach one foot out to the side. Step back together, then reach the opposite foot out. 

3. Repeat this movement, while trying not to move up and down with your body.

exercises.name: Mini Band Around Arms Bear Crawl
exercises.description:
Place a mini band around your wrists and get into a bear crawl position with knees hovering.

Crawl forward and backward, keeping tension in the band and maintaining a stable core throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Mini Band Backward Fencing Walk
exercises.description:
1. Start by placing the band around the arches of both of your feet. 

2. Slightly bend your knees and step one foot backwards while keeping the band tense. 

3. Slowly step your front foot back towards your rear foot, then repeat, travelling backwards.

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Mini Band Barbell Back Squat
exercises.description:
1) Wrap a Miniband around your thighs, just above the knee.

2) With the Barbell across your upper back, set your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Have your feet turned out slightly.

2) Keep your chest up and knees out as you sit back and down into your hips. Don't let your knees collapse inward.

3) Under control, reverse the motion and push yourself back up to a standing position.

exercises.name: Mini Band Barbell Front Squat
exercises.description:
1) Place a miniband around your thighs, just above the knee.

2) With the Barbell right above your chest, set your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Have a slight turn out of your toes. 

3) From there, you’ll sit your hips back, keeping your knees out.

4) Reverse the motion by pushing the floor away and returning to the start position.

exercises.name: Mini Band Bear Crawl (Around Arms)
exercises.description:
1. Start with the band looped around both wrists, on all fours, with knees slightly elevated. 

2. In a slow and controlled manner, walk forwards. Reach your opposite arm and leg at the same time, without wagging hips from side to side. 

3. Repeat in reverse, if desired.

exercises.name: Mini Band Bear Crawl (Around Legs)
exercises.description:
1. Start with the band looped around both knees, on all fours, with knees slightly elevated. 

2. In a slow and controlled manner, walk forwards. Reach your opposite arm and leg at the same time, without wagging hips from side to side. 

3. Repeat in reverse if desired.

exercises.name: Mini Band Bent Arm Pull Apart
exercises.description:
Hold a mini band with both hands, arms bent at 90 degrees, and elbows close to your sides. 
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Pull the band apart by moving your hands outward while keeping your elbows bent and stable. 

Slowly return to the starting position. 

This exercise targets the shoulders, upper back, and arms.

exercises.name: Mini Band Bent Over Y's
exercises.description:
Place a mini band around your wrists and stand with feet hip-width apart. 

Raise your arms overhead in a "Y" shape, keeping tension in the band. Lower your arms back down with control, and repeat the movement.

exercises.name: Mini Band Bicycle Crunch
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on your back, with the Mini Band looped around the middle of your feet. 

2. Bring your feet up off the ground, and bend your knees to 90-degrees. 

3. Push one foot away while bringing the opposite foot towards you. Lift your torso up off the ground, and gently rotate towards the knee closest to you. 

4. Slowly lower yourself back down and repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Mini Band Burpee
exercises.description:
1. Place the band around the arches of both your feet. 

2. Reach down to place your hands on the floor, then jump back into a plank position. 

3. Lower your chest to the floor, then push yourself back up the plank.

4. Jump your feet back up towards your hands, stand tall and jump while reaching over head.

exercises.name: Mini Band Bus Drivers
exercises.description: Hold a mini band taut in front of you and twist it side to side as if turning a steering wheel.
